claim: Phi1 is the dominant residual mode in S(I,T) after PT backbone subtraction
metric used: Mean holdout RMSE PT-only vs PT+kappa1*Phi1
limitation: Uses canonical low-T strip protocol; reflects this decomposition pipeline

claim: Phi1 explains most predictive gain in full LOOCV hierarchy
metric used: Aggregate LOOCV RMSE PT-only vs rank-1
limitation: n=12 PT-valid rows in this cohort

claim: Rank-1 structure is universal across the canonical T window, with known boundary weakening
quantitative result: corr_A mostly 0.99 at 4-20 K; drops to 0.7469 at 22 K; 0.7997 at 30 K
limitation: Universality is strong but not uniform; boundary rows are weaker

claim: Rank-2 is real but subleading relative to Phi1
limitation: Confirms subleading structure, not Phi1 insufficiency

claim: Pure local shift is not the Phi1 mechanism
quantitative result: median corr(deltaS,tangent)=-0.4760 vs rank-2 corr=0.9844; verdict RESIDUAL_IS_LOCAL_SHIFT=NO
limitation: Test targets shift hypothesis directly; not a complete kernel taxonomy

claim: Pure nonlinear response of Scdf is not sufficient
metric used: Mean RMSE nonlinear Scdf model vs rank-1/2
limitation: Model family limited to Scdf and Scdf^2 terms

claim: Phi1 is not reducible to PT-only or simple geometric kernels
quantitative result: All simple kernel families tested underperform rank-1 Phi1 residual model
limitation: Evidence is from tested kernel families, not exhaustive over all possible kernels

claim: Phi1 amplitude (kappa1) is stable and observable-linked across T
quantitative result: best LOOCV 0.0184739 (width+S_peak); excluding 22-24 K LOOCV 0.0146342; alignment delta ~3.16e-16
limitation: This is amplitude stability; does not directly prove full shape invariance

claim: Phi1 corresponds to broad symmetric ridge correction in map language
quantitative result: Independent artifacts converge on 'dominant broad symmetric ridge correction vs PT backbone'
limitation: Symmetry claim for Phi1 is mostly qualitative; no dedicated stored phi1 even/odd scalar table found

claim: PT deformation coordinates can capture part of residual structure but do not replace Phi1-centered rank description
quantitative result: deform3 mean RMSE 0.00656 (better than rank-1) but worse than rank-2 0.00567; phi2 span(K1,K2) cosine=0.414
limitation: Metrics are for Phi2/deformation closure; indirect for Phi1 interpretation

claim: Collapse/boundary failure structure is concentrated near 22-24 K and does not invalidate Phi1 dominance
metric used: Boundary anomalies in correlations and residual concentration
quantitative result: rank-1 corr dip at 22 K (0.7469); alpha_res RMS band 0.6717 vs outside 0.3856
limitation: Boundary evidence partly from alpha channel; not a dedicated standalone Phi1-collapse scalar
